input.question: How do I enable duplex printing?

documents.text: Duplex printing: open Settings > Print > Duplex and toggle it on. Duplex is supported on PrintFlow PF-300 and later models.
The PrintFlow app is available for iOS and Android.

input.question: What is the default print resolution and can I change it?

documents.text: The default print resolution is 300 dpi. It can be raised to 1200 dpi under Settings > Print > Quality.
Supported paper sizes: A4, Letter and Legal. The rear tray accepts envelopes.

input.question: How do I connect the printer to Wi-Fi?

documents.text: To connect to Wi-Fi, press and hold the WPS button for 3 seconds, or use the PrintFlow app under Device > Network.
The status LED blinks blue while pairing.

input.question: How do I clear a paper jam?

documents.text: Paper jam (error E13): open the rear tray, pull the sheet out slowly in the paper path direction, then close the tray. The E13 error clears automatically.
Never pull jammed paper against the feed direction.

input.question: What ink cartridges does the PF-500 use?

documents.text: The PrintFlow PF-500 uses the INK-52 black cartridge and the INK-53 tri-color cartridge.

input.question: How do I update the firmware?

documents.text: Firmware updates are installed from the PrintFlow app: Device > About > Firmware > Update. The printer must stay powered on during the update.
Firmware release notes are published on the support site.

input.question: Can I print from my phone?

documents.text: Mobile printing is supported through the PrintFlow app on iOS and Android, or via standard AirPrint.
The app requires the phone and printer to share a network.
